content.problemStatement:
Write a C program to demonstrate how a pointer can be decremented to move to the previous memory location. You will be given an array of integers and an index. You need to print the value at the given index and the value at the index before it using pointer decrement.


Input Format

The first line contains the size of the array `n`.
The second line contains `n` space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.
The third line contains an integer `index`.


Output Format

The first line should print the value at the given `index`.
The second line should print the value at the `index - 1`.
constraints: Array elements fit in a 32‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 15), the size of the array. Second line contains n space‑separated 32‑bit signed integers. Third line contains an integer index (1‑based, 1 ≤ index ≤ n).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the value at the given index on a new line. If a previous element exists (index > 1), print the value at index‑1 on the next line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Array is [10,20,30,40,50]; index=3 (1‑based). Element at position 3 is 30; previous element (position 2) is 20.
Array [1,2,3]; index=2 gives element 2 and the previous element 1.
Only one element exists. The value at index 1 is 42. No previous element, so only one line is printed.

solution.approach: Read the array and the required 1‑based index. The element at the index is arr[index‑1]. If index > 1, the previous element is arr[index‑2]. Using a pointer is only a demonstration; in Java we directly access the array. The algorithm is O(n) per test case for reading the input and O(1) for answering the query.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case (dominated by reading the n elements). Overall O(total N) for all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) per test case to store the array.
